studyName: OncoArray: Follow-up of Ovarian Cancer Genetic Association and Interaction Studies (FOCI)

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject IDs, primary cancer site, tumor behavior, grade and histology, country of study site, ethnicity, age, GWAS exclusion, and principal components 1-9 (European), 1-10 (Asian), 1 (African), and 1-6 (Other) used in association analyses.

variables.name: BEHAVIOR
variables.description: Tumor behavior

variables.name: COUNTRY
variables.description: Country of study site

variables.name: ETHNICITY
variables.description: Intercontinental ancestry estimated from genotypes

variables.name: GRADE
variables.description: Tumor grade

variables.name: GWAS_EXCLUDE
variables.description: Indicator if analyzed in OCAC GWAS

variables.name: HISTOTYPE
variables.description: Tumor histology

variables.name: PRIMARY_SITE
variables.description: Primary cancer site or control status

variables.name: REF_AGE
variables.description: Age at diagnosis (cases) or interview (controls)

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: De-identified Subject ID

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject IDs, consent information, sex, and subject aliases.

variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.description: Biological sex

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample IDs, body site where sample was collected, and analyte type.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte Type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: De-identified Sample ID

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
